title: differentialModeChoke
description: The description of a Differential Mode Choke (DMC) for EMI filtering. Differential mode chokes use an inductor to attenuate differential mode noise (noise between line and neutral or between lines). Unlike CMCs, DMCs present impedance to differential signals and are typically used in combination with capacitors to form LC filters.

inputVoltage.description: The input voltage of the filter stage

operatingCurrent.description: The nominal operating current through the choke

lineFrequency.description: The mains/line frequency in Hz (50 or 60 Hz typically). This is the frequency of the load current that causes magnetic excitation.

minimumInductance.description: The minimum required inductance in henries for the filter cutoff frequency

filterCapacitance.description: The filter capacitance in Farads (for LC filter design)

minimumImpedance.description: The minimum impedance requirements at specified frequencies

targetAttenuation.description: Target attenuation in dB at specified frequencies

switchingFrequency.description: The switching frequency of the converter being filtered

maximumDcResistance.description: Maximum allowable DC resistance in ohms

maximumCoreTemperatureRise.description: Maximum allowable temperature rise due to core and winding losses

ambientTemperature.description: The ambient temperature for the application

attenuationAtFrequency.description: Attenuation requirement at a specific frequency
attenuationAtFrequency.title: attenuationAtFrequency

attenuation.description: The target attenuation in dB at this frequency
